title: Eva Rampage
wikitext:
[]: Eva Rampage             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Oct 25, 2008 9:55 pm Could NERV have stopped an Eva had one of them gone berserk and decide to tear up half of Tokyo-3? I mean a regular Eva, not that mess with Bardiel.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Oct 25, 2008 11:03 pm They used a restraint system to capture 01 after it ate Zeruel, so yes. ...why didn't they try to use that on the angel though, I wonder?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 25, 2008 11:23 pm they only did that in the manga, which I thought was dumb lowest common denominator I thought the anime version was far more intriguing; it just mysteriously stopped completely, as suddenly as it has started.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 26, 2008 12:06 am Correction: They show us nothing about how the Eva returned to the cage in the Anime. First it's out in the open, free from Nerv's control, berserk and bellowing, and next episode it's in the cage, covered in bandages. What happened in the meantime? No explanation. The Manga implies that Tokyo-3's defenses were meant to stop a rampaging Eva as much as keep an Angel out. I assume the idea was that Nerv knew how to stop a rampaging Eva, while they knew nothing about what attacking Angels would be like. Anime: Cop-out, Manga: Fridge logic.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Oct 26, 2008 12:30 am Maybe Yui is just more obedient than we previously thought. After all, the threat has passed, she already finished eating the angel. Why resist when she is damage from her battle and has her son right where she wants him?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Oct 26, 2008 11:43 am Giant Net> Unit 01? I don't see it somehow. "Behold it has become a GOD! Release the Net of Longinus!" Seems silly. Although the anime plot holes' just as annoying.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Oct 26, 2008 12:38 pm FO wrote: Maybe Yui is just more obedient than we previously thought. After all, the threat has passed, she already finished eating the angel. Why resist when she is damage from her battle and has her son right where she wants him?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 26, 2008 4:36 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Correction: They show us nothing about how the Eva returned to the cage in the Anime. First it's out in the open, free from Nerv's control, berserk and bellowing, and next episode it's in the cage, covered in bandages. What happened in the meantime? No explanation. The Manga implies that Tokyo-3's defenses were meant to stop a rampaging Eva as much as keep an Angel out. I assume the idea was that Nerv knew how to stop a rampaging Eva, while they knew nothing about what attacking Angels would be like. Anime: Cop-out, Manga: Fridge logic.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun Oct 26, 2008 5:55 pm It might literally have done just that. It had nothing else to do, so why keep going berserk? If it had shut down, recovering it would've been relatively easy. No different from recovering another EVA in anyway's.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Thu Oct 30, 2008 7:05 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Ima go with this line of thought. Yui found what she is looking for: the organ and her son. Perhaps she is victoriously laughing in NERV's face instead of just yelling around. But what about her obedience during 3I? Was she that confidant that her son was going to reject 3I that she just played along until the very end?

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Nov 02, 2008 8:30 pm I think you're saying a lot based on just Eva-01. Number of berserks aside, I don't know if we can use her for a standard. BTW, I thought she just shut down after eating Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Nov 02, 2008 10:34 pm BTW, I thought she just shut down after eating Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Mon Nov 03, 2008 2:08 am Well, my opinion is that with the S2 Organ consumed, and the angel dead, yui decided to not resist capture. Nerv wanted to be safe, so they captured it anyway, and probably due to the disappearance of Shinji as well.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 03, 2008 2:39 pm Action_Bastard wrote: red goo

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Nov 03, 2008 2:49 pm Point taken master_lloyd, my previous post was edited accordingly. Thank you for pointing that out.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Nov 03, 2008 9:27 pm To be fair, the Bakelite in NGE is not the same as real bakelite. The real stuff isn't red, flowy goo. Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic, which means that it doesn't melt once heated. Once made out of melamine (I forget the molecular structure) and Formaldehyde (HCHO for all chemistry nerds), real bakelite stays hard until it is decomposed by intense heat and can't be reformed.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Nov 04, 2008 12:17 am I knew that already, but, um...thanks Ursus, for pointing that out as well. But I obviously meant the stuff from the show of course. Just wanted to reiterate that for those who are late in reading this thread.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Nov 04, 2008 9:39 pm I just rewatched Eva-01 slaughtering Bardiel and Zeruel. I still find it hard to believe that NERV could stop something that powerful if it decided to tear up Tokyo-3

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 6:24 pm NERV could only stop an Eva with another Eva. Or with Rei and her angel superpowers.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Nov 16, 2008 10:00 pm Count A'ight wrote: NERV could only stop an Eva with another Eva. Or with Rei and her angel superpowers.
